input_candidates.prediction_value: Solution treatment was conducted on a hot-rolled 4 mm sheet obtained by forging a 30 mm × 30 mm billet from a 50 kg vacuum-melted ingot.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fatigue crack propagation zone area fractions (fa) were measured on SEM images and labelled in Fig. 6; numerical values are not given in the text.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Tensile tests: room temperature, constant strain rate of 10^-3 s^-1, tensile axis parallel to rolling direction, Shimadzu AG-Xplus 250 kN.

input_candidates.truth_value: EBSD step size used for crack path analysis was 0.8 μm.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fatigue tests: stress-controlled tension-tension, sinusoidal waveform, 20 Hz, stress ratio R = 0.1, ambient air, Shimadzu EHF-EV050K2A-020-1, loading axis parallel to rolling direction.

input_candidates.truth_value: Precipitation strengthening contributions (Δσ_cs, Δσ_ms, Δσ_os) are modelled but not numerically quantified for these conditions.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Table 1 provides uniform elongation (UE) and total elongation (TE); UE values are 57.7 ± 1.3 % (ST), 45.5 ± 1.4 % (550-6), 45.1 ± 1.5 % (600-6), 46.4 ± 1.2 % (650-6); TE values are 72.0 ± 0.8 %, 57.7 ± 0.4 %, 58.4 ± 1.4 %, 52.7 ± 1.6 %, respectively.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fatigue tests were performed under stress control with a sinusoidal waveform at 20 Hz, stress ratio R=0.1.

The dominant deformation mechanism in all samples is planar dislocation slip; in ST it is associated with short-range ordering, while in aged samples it is enhanced by κ-carbide shearing (glide plane softening).
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 4 tabulates engineering stress values (MPa) at engineering strains of 0 %, 20 %, 40 %, 60 %, and 70 % for all four samples, but continuous stress-strain curve data are not provided in text.

input_candidates.truth_value: A critical κ-carbide size for transition from shearing to Orowan bypassing is reported as ~27 nm based on literature; all samples here remain in the shearing regime.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 5 presents Δσ/2-2Nf curves in double-logarithmic coordinates; fatigue strengths at 10^7 cycles are 157.5 MPa (ST), 162 MPa (550-6), 175.5 MPa (600-6), and 171 MPa (650-6).

input_candidates.truth_value: No quantitative data on density, hardness, or thermal/electrical properties were reported.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 6 macroscopic fracture surfaces show three-zone morphology (crack initiation, propagation, rapid fracture); the area fraction of crack initiation and propagation zones is larger in aged samples than in ST, with 600-6 largest at 180 MPa and 550-6 highest at 315 MPa, consistent with optimal fatigue resistance at low and high stress amplitudes respectively.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 5 displays Δσ/2-2Nf curves; exact data points are not extractable from the text.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 7 fatigue crack propagation zones exhibit well-defined fatigue striations indicating transgranular crack advancement; 550-6 and 600-6 show more pronounced and closely spaced striations, with narrowest spacing in 550-6 at 315 MPa and in 600-6 at 180 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: The fatigue strength improvement percentages relative to ST were calculated as approximately 2.9% (550-6), 11.4% (600-6), and 8.6% (650-6) based on stress amplitudes at 10^7 cycles.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 8 rapid fracture zones: ST, 550-6, and 600-6 show dimples indicating ductile microvoid coalescence; 650-6 displays mixed dimples and cleavage facets attributed to extensive intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.
